title: clusia criuva subsp. sellowiana
intro: Clusia criuva subsp. sellowiana, also known as the Brazilian tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Clusiaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and protecting against certain infections. In herbal preparations, the leaves of Clusia criuva subsp. sellowiana are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
